summary: Restructured the WIN-060 post-hoc paragraph to lead with the public-availability argument (~390-year-old observable since Halley) and demote monitor.py absence to a symptom of the disqualifier. Single find/replace patch on WIN-060.detail_verdict_text. Curmudgeon-flagged circularity (defender opening "you penalize us for not monitoring") is closed because the disqualifier no longer rests on monitoring absence — it now rests on the legitimate-novelty carve-out (Part 3.1b) being unsatisfiable for a 390-year-old indexed observable. Broader 13-WIN template-share (WIN-001/013/022/033/040/041/042/043/048/052/054/066/067) is documented in scope_note as deferred follow-up sweep — DO NOT extend EXP-295 patches there; each per-WIN observation-age lead needs individual fact-checking.
